item.title: Language, power and the fear of borrowed words
item.description: In recent months, a curious linguistic anxiety has gripped Bangladesh. Words like ‘azadi’, ‘inquilab’, ‘insaf’, ‘bayan’, ‘bandobast’, ‘majlum’ and ‘faisala’ have migrated from slogans and speeches into television talk shows and Facebook posts. They echo in political rallies and throb beneath red, green and sometimes black banners. Suddenly, everyone has become a guardian of the mother tongue......
